user.title: Bosch Smart Home Camera — Login (Step 1 of 2)
user.description:
**How to log in:**

1. Copy the URL from the field below
2. Open it in your browser
3. Log in with your **Bosch SingleKey ID**
4. Browser shows a **404 page** — this is expected
5. Copy the **full URL** from the address bar
6. Click **Submit** to continue

data_description.login_url: Copy this URL and open it in your browser to log in.

auth.title: Bosch Smart Home Camera — Paste URL (Step 2 of 2)
auth.description:
Paste the redirect URL from your browser address bar below.

**Important:** The code expires after ~60 seconds.

data_description.redirect_url: The full URL from the browser address bar after login (contains the authorization code)

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate Bosch Smart Home Camera
reauth_confirm.description:
Your Bosch Cloud token has expired and cannot be refreshed automatically. This usually happens when the refresh token was invalidated on Bosch's side.

Click **Submit** to open the Bosch SingleKey ID login in your browser. After login you'll be redirected back to Home Assistant automatically and your existing configuration (entities, automations, options) will be preserved.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Bosch Smart Home Camera
reconfigure.description: Run the OAuth login again to refresh your credentials without removing the integration. All existing entities, automations, and options are preserved.

error.invalid_redirect_url: No auth code found in the URL.
error.token_exchange_failed: Login failed — the code may have expired. Try again.

abort.reauth_successful: Re-authentication successful — your Bosch Smart Home Camera integration has been updated with a fresh token. All entities, automations, and options are preserved.

data.mark_events_read: Mark Bosch cloud events as read
data.high_quality_video: High quality video (30 Mbps) — default for stream quality
data.fcm_push_mode: FCM Push Mode (auto / android / ios / polling)
data.audio_default_on: Audio ON by default when starting stream
data.debug_logging: Debug logging (verbose TLS proxy & stream logs)

data_description.scan_interval: How often the integration fetches data from the Bosch cloud. Lower = faster, more API calls. Default: 60
data_description.interval_status: How often cameras are pinged for ONLINE/OFFLINE. Default: 300 (5 min)
data_description.interval_events: How often to check for new motion/audio events. With FCM Push this is only a fallback. Default: 300 (5 min)
data_description.snapshot_interval: How often to proactively refresh camera snapshots. Range: 300–86400. Default: 1800 (30 min)
data_description.enable_snapshots: Show the latest motion-triggered JPEG as a camera entity in HA.
data_description.enable_sensors: Create sensor entities for camera status, WiFi signal, firmware, events today, last event, etc.
data_description.enable_snapshot_button: Create switch and button entities: Live Stream, Audio, Privacy Mode, Camera Light, Notifications, Refresh.
data_description.enable_binary_sensors: Create binary sensors that turn ON for 30 seconds after a motion or audio alarm event. Useful for automations.
data_description.download_path: Save new event snapshots (JPEG) and video clips (MP4) to this local folder when an FCM push arrives. Leave empty to disable local saving. Default: /config/bosch_events
data_description.media_browser_source:
Filters which backend the Media Browser entry **Bosch SHC Camera** shows.
• **Auto** (default): show every backend that has data — local save folder and/or NAS upload.
• **Local only**: hide the NAS even if SMB upload is active.
• **NAS only**: hide local files even if local save is configured.
• **Disabled**: do not expose the Media Browser entry at all.
data_description.enable_fcm_push: Receive motion/audio events in ~2 seconds via Firebase Cloud Messaging instead of polling every 5 minutes. Automatic fallback to polling if FCM goes down. Recommended!
data_description.alert_notify_service: Default fallback: used when the per-type fields below are empty. Comma-separated. Examples: notify.signal_messenger, notify.mobile_app_xxx. Leave empty to disable alerts.
data_description.alert_notify_system: Services for system alerts (token expired, disk full). Empty = uses default fallback above.
data_description.alert_notify_information: Services for step 1 — instant text notification. Empty = default fallback. e.g.: notify.signal_messenger, notify.mobile_app_xxx.
data_description.alert_notify_screenshot: Services for step 2 — snapshot image. Empty = skip this step (no fallback). Mobile apps (mobile_app_xxx) receive the image inline; others as file attachment.
data_description.alert_notify_video: Services for step 3 — video clip. Empty = skip this step (no fallback).
data_description.alert_save_snapshots: Keep downloaded event images and videos in www/bosch_alerts/ (inside the HA config dir). If OFF, files are deleted within seconds after sending.
data_description.alert_delete_after_send: Remove local files after the notification service has received them. Only applies when 'Save locally' is OFF.
data_description.mark_events_read: When OFF (default), new motion/audio events stay flagged as unread in the Bosch app — even after HA processes them — so the app keeps highlighting them as new. Turn ON to make the integration mark events as read on the Bosch cloud after processing (the previous behaviour, useful when you treat HA as the primary client and don't want the app to show stale 'new event' badges).
data_description.high_quality_video: Set the default stream quality to 30 Mbps (instead of Auto ~7.5 Mbps). Can be overridden at runtime via the 'Video Quality' select entity.
data_description.stream_connection_type:
Default connection type for live streams.
• **auto** (recommended): Try local LAN first, automatically fall back to Bosch cloud proxy on failure.
• **local**: Direct LAN only — no internet required. Faster RTSP, but slower snapshots (~6–10 s). Session auto-renewed every 50 s.
• **remote**: Always via Bosch cloud proxy. Faster snapshots (~0.4–1.9 s). Best default when HA is not on the same LAN.
The 'Stream Mode' select entity overrides this setting at runtime without restart.
data_description.live_buffer_mode:
How many seconds of video the player pre-buffers before showing it. More buffer = fewer stutters but larger lag behind real time.
• **Latency**: small buffer (~4-6 s lag). May stutter on flaky Wi-Fi.
• **Balanced** (recommended): medium buffer (~8-10 s). Robust against typical Wi-Fi hiccups.
• **Stable**: large buffer (~12-15 s). Smooth even on weak links, but noticeably delayed.
Affects only the card display — has no effect on the camera, recording, or snapshots.
data_description.shc_ip: Smart Home Controller IP for local API features (e.g. 192.168.1.100). Optional — most features work without SHC.
data_description.shc_cert_path: Path to client certificate PEM file for SHC mutual TLS. Example: /config/shc_cert.pem
data_description.shc_key_path: Path to client key PEM file for SHC mutual TLS. Example: /config/shc_key.pem
data_description.fcm_push_mode: Choose push mode. Auto tries iOS first, then Android, then falls back to polling. Polling uses standard API calls only (no FCM).
data_description.audio_default_on: When ON: audio switch starts enabled (stream with sound). When OFF: audio starts muted. Can be toggled anytime in the dashboard.
data_description.enable_intercom: Enable the intercom (two-way audio) switch entity. Disabled by default. Allows sending audio to the camera speaker.
data_description.enable_go2rtc: Automatically register live streams with HA's bundled go2rtc for WebRTC playback (~2 s latency vs. ~10 s HLS). Recommended. Disable only if you run a custom go2rtc setup.
data_description.enable_smb_upload: Upload event snapshots and video clips to an SMB/CIFS or FTP network share (e.g. FRITZ!Box NAS, Synology, any Windows share). SMB requires the smbprotocol package; FTP uses Python's built-in ftplib.
data_description.upload_protocol: Transfer protocol. **SMB** (default) is the typical Windows/macOS share. **FTP** is significantly faster on many NAS devices (e.g. FRITZ!Box) for lots of small files and sidesteps the known macOS-Sequoia SMB-rename hang. With FTP the share-name field is ignored; the base path is relative to the FTP root (e.g. `FILES/Bosch-Kameras` instead of just `Bosch-Kameras`).
data_description.smb_server: IP address or hostname. Example: 192.168.1.1 (FRITZ!Box), nas.local or fritz.box
data_description.smb_share: Name of the SMB share (SMB only). Example: FRITZ.NAS
data_description.smb_base_path: Base folder on the share where files are stored. Default: Bosch-Kameras
data_description.smb_file_pattern: File name pattern (without extension). Variables: {camera}, {date}, {time}, {type}, {id}, {year}, {month}, {day}. Default: {camera}_{date}_{time}_{type}_{id}
data_description.smb_retention_days: Automatically delete files on the NAS older than this many days. 0 = keep forever. Default: 180 (6 months). Runs once per day.
data_description.smb_disk_warn_mb: Send an alert when free space on the NAS share falls below this threshold in MB. 0 = disabled. Default: 5120 MB (5 GB). Uses configured notify service, falls back to HA notification.
data_description.enable_nvr: Continuously record each camera's LOCAL stream to disk in 5-minute MP4 segments. LAN-only — recording pauses if the camera is on the cloud relay. Adds a recording switch per camera (disabled by default; enable each camera individually). Requires ffmpeg.
data_description.nvr_storage_target: Where the finalized NVR segments end up. **Local**: stays under the storage folder below. **SMB / FTP**: ffmpeg still writes locally first (staging), then a watcher uploads each segment to the same NAS configured in the events-storage section, under a separate subfolder. Default: Local.
data_description.nvr_base_path: Folder for NVR segments. Each camera gets one subfolder, then one subfolder per day, with 5-minute MP4 segments named HH-MM.mp4 inside. Default: /config/bosch_nvr. Use a NAS mount for >1 day retention — at 5 Mbps a single camera writes ~54 GB/day.
data_description.nvr_smb_subpath: Subfolder under the SMB / FTP base path used exclusively for NVR segments. Default: NVR. Keeps the NVR tree separate from cloud-event uploads.
data_description.nvr_retention_days: Delete NVR segments older than this many days. Range: 1-365. Default: 3. Runs once per day.
data_description.debug_logging: Enable verbose logging for TLS proxy RTSP exchanges, stream timing, and pre-warm diagnostics. Useful for troubleshooting LOCAL stream issues. Disable for normal use.
data_description.force_relogin: Check this to re-authenticate via browser. Only needed if the refresh token has expired (very rare).
data_description.migrate_to_oss_client: Switch from the legacy `residential_app` client to the dedicated `oss_residential_app` client Bosch provides for open source integrations. Enabling this will trigger the reauth flow — you will be asked to log in once via Bosch SingleKey ID, and your existing entities, automations, and options will be preserved.

polling.name: Polling intervals
polling.description: How often the integration talks to the Bosch cloud.

features.name: Features
features.description: Toggle the entity types the integration creates.

stream.name: Live stream
stream.description: Connection mode and player buffering for live video.

fcm.name: Push notifications
fcm.description: Real-time event delivery via FCM and per-step notify services.

events_storage.name: Event storage (download / SMB / FTP)
events_storage.description: Save Bosch cloud event JPEGs and MP4 clips locally or on a NAS share.

nvr.name: Mini-NVR
nvr.description: Continuous LAN-only recording. Optional drain to SMB or FTP.

shc.name: Smart Home Controller (optional)
shc.description: Local SHC API for camera light + privacy mode without cloud.

auth.name: Authentication
auth.description: Force a fresh browser login or migrate to the OSS OAuth client.

relogin_show.title: Re-login — Step 1 of 2
relogin_show.description: Copy the URL below, open in browser, log in, then click Submit.

data_description.login_url: Copy and open in your browser.

relogin_paste.title: Re-login — Step 2 of 2
relogin_paste.description: Paste the redirect URL. **Code expires in ~60 seconds!**

data_description.redirect_url: Full URL from browser address bar after login (contains the authorization code)

error.invalid_redirect_url: No auth code found. Paste the full URL.

abort.migration_started: Migration started — please complete the Reconfigure flow on the integration card.

trigger_snapshot.description: Force an immediate snapshot refresh for all Bosch cameras.

open_live_connection.description: Open a live stream proxy connection (H.264 30fps + AAC audio).

camera_id.name: Camera ID
camera_id.description: Cloud UUID of the camera (find in entity attributes)

live_connection_failed.message: Could not establish live connection for camera {cam_id_prefix} — see log for mitmproxy capture instructions

privacy_blocked.message: {action}: feature unavailable while Privacy mode is active

token_expired.title: Bosch Camera — Token expired

auth_server_outage.title: Bosch Camera — Auth server outage
auth_server_outage.description:
The Bosch authentication server is currently returning HTTP {error}. This is a Bosch-side problem, not a configuration issue on your side.

The integration will retry automatically (exponential backoff up to 10 minutes). Entities are unavailable until the server recovers — no action needed.
